assertions.title: Local scores are computed and persisted in the report

steps.action: Inspect src/ornn_bench/scoring.py and the scoring-focused tests
steps.expected: The run flow should compute Ornn-I/Ornn-T, qualification, score_status, and helpful score_status_detail when metrics are partial or missing
steps.observed: derive_scores_from_sections() builds ScoreResult via aggregate_gpu_scores(); _build_score_detail() augments partial/error cases with source-specific detail

steps.expected: A completed scored run produces numeric ornn_i/ornn_t, valid score_status, qualification, and serialized score components
steps.observed: PASSED — report.scores had ornn_i=100.0, ornn_t=100.0, score_status=VALID, qualification=Premium, aggregate_method=minimum, and two per_gpu_scores records

steps.expected: When a required section fails, the saved report should persist a partial/error score with actionable detail
steps.observed: PASSED — missing memory produced score_status partial, ornn_i null, ornn_t 100.0, aggregate_method minimum, and detail text mentioning memory and bw

steps.expected: The CLI run command writes the computed scores into the output JSON report
steps.observed: PASSED — the generated JSON contained ornn_i 100.0, ornn_t 100.0, qualification Premium, score_status valid, aggregate_method minimum, and both GPU UUIDs under per_gpu_scores

assertions.title: Score components are normalized ratios suitable for server verification

steps.expected: Persisted components should be normalized against documented H100 references so server verification can use refs=1.0
steps.observed: SECTION_METRIC_REFS defines bw/fp8/bf16/ar anchors and derive_scores_from_sections() documents that persisted components are ratio-based for server recomputation

steps.expected: Reference-level fixture metrics should normalize to component ratios of 1.0
steps.observed: PASSED — report.scores.components equaled bw=1.0, fp8=1.0, bf16=1.0, ar=1.0

steps.expected: Non-reference fixture metrics should be persisted as ratios and server recomputation should match within tolerance
steps.observed: PASSED — fp8 persisted as 1600.0/1782.6, bf16 as 800.0/891.3, and recompute_and_verify() returned VERIFIED

steps.expected: The client should submit normalized components and local scores to the verification endpoint
steps.observed: OrnnApiClient.verify() posts components, ornn_i, ornn_t, and qualification to /api/v1/verify

assertions.title: Multi-GPU aggregation uses minimum-GPU semantics and is transparent

steps.expected: Aggregate Ornn-I/Ornn-T should use minimum-GPU semantics and preserve per-GPU transparency
steps.observed: aggregate_gpu_scores() computes per-GPU records, aggregates with min() across valid GPU scores, and returns aggregate_method='minimum' with per_gpu_scores populated

steps.expected: A weaker GPU should gate the aggregate scores
steps.observed: PASSED — with GPU ratios 1.0 and 0.5, aggregate Ornn-I and Ornn-T were both 50.0

steps.expected: Per-GPU records and explicit aggregate_method should be retained
steps.observed: PASSED — per_gpu_scores preserved GPU-AAA and GPU-BBB, and aggregate_method was recorded as minimum

steps.expected: The saved JSON report should expose aggregate_method and per_gpu_scores for transparency
steps.observed: PASSED — output JSON stored aggregate_method minimum and two per_gpu_scores entries with GPU UUIDs

assertions.title: Interconnect AR component derives from a documented NCCL metric

steps.expected: The AR component should use a single documented NCCL source metric
steps.observed: AR_COMPONENT_SOURCE is interconnect.bus_bandwidth_summary.all_reduce_1gb.avg_busbw, with fallback to nccl_results.all_reduce_1gb.avg_bus_bandwidth if the summary field is absent

steps.expected: The fixture should distinguish the intended all_reduce_1gb metric from other NCCL metrics
steps.observed: The fixture sets all_reduce_1gb to 148.32 and also includes an unrelated all_reduce_sweep value of 999.0

steps.expected: AR normalization should come from the documented all_reduce_1gb field, not the sweep metric
steps.observed: PASSED — components.ar was exactly 1.0, which matches 148.32/148.32 and would not be possible if the 999.0 sweep metric had been used

assertions.title: Compute BF16/FP8 components derive from per-GPU MAMF best results

steps.expected: BF16 and FP8 components should come from per-GPU MAMF best.tflops values
steps.observed: BF16_COMPONENT_SOURCE points to compute.per_gpu.<gpu>.bf16.best.tflops, FP8_COMPONENT_SOURCES point to fp8_e4m3/fp8_e5m2 best.tflops, and _best_tflops() extracts the best.tflops field

steps.expected: The fixture should supply per-GPU BF16 and FP8 MAMF best values
steps.observed: Each GPU fixture entry contains bf16.best.tflops plus fp8_e4m3.best.tflops and fp8_e5m2.best.tflops

steps.expected: Reference-level MAMF best.tflops values should normalize BF16 and FP8 to 1.0
steps.observed: PASSED — report.scores.components contained bf16=1.0 and fp8=1.0

steps.expected: Changing per-GPU best.tflops values should change the persisted BF16/FP8 ratios deterministically
steps.observed: PASSED — fp8 persisted as 1600.0/1782.6 and bf16 as 800.0/891.3, then verified successfully server-side

assertions.title: Memory BW component derives from nvbandwidth results deterministically

steps.expected: The BW component should come from a single documented nvbandwidth field
steps.observed: BW_COMPONENT_SOURCE is memory.nvbandwidth_results.device_local_copy.max and _extract_memory_bw_ratio() reads that field directly

steps.expected: The fixture should include the documented field and distracting alternatives so the source field can be proven
steps.observed: The fixture sets device_local_copy.max=2039.5, while device_local_copy_sm.max=9999.0 and d2d_bidir.max=4079.0 are also present

steps.expected: BW normalization should use device_local_copy.max and therefore equal 1.0 at the reference fixture value
steps.observed: PASSED — components.bw was 1.0, confirming the documented source field was used instead of the larger alternative metrics

steps.expected: If the memory section is unavailable, the scoring detail should explicitly call out BW/memory as missing
steps.observed: PASSED — the partial score detail mentioned both memory and bw

assertions.title: Server verification can recompute and match local scores

steps.expected: Server verification should recompute scores from submitted normalized components using the same formulas and tolerance semantics as the CLI
steps.observed: api.scoring.recompute_and_verify() uses refs=1.0 and DEFAULT_TOLERANCE=0.01, while OrnnApiClient.verify() posts components, local scores, and qualification to /api/v1/verify

steps.expected: A locally scored report built from normalized components should verify successfully
steps.observed: PASSED — recompute_and_verify() returned VERIFIED for the locally derived report components and scores

steps.expected: Exact matches and small deltas within tolerance should verify
steps.observed: PASSED — exact scores verified, and 100.005/99.995 also verified within the default 0.01 tolerance

summary: Tested 7 assertions: 7 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. Targeted scoring validation passed across 149 broad pytest checks and 9 focused scoring/verification cases. Local scoring persisted normalized components, partial-score detail, and minimum-based per-GPU aggregates, and the server recomputation path matched local scores within tolerance.
